qa_pairs.question: How do I gain access to the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users must opt-in on the CCDB webpage at https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems.

qa_pairs.question: Who was automatically opted-in to the Trillium cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users who had opted into Niagara/Mist on August 5, 2025, were automatically opted-in to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: Does opting into Trillium give me access to both CPU and GPU resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, opting into Trillium grants access to both the CPU and GPU subclusters.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get access to HPSS, Trillium's nearline system?
qa_pairs.answer: There is a separate opt-in for HPSS on the CCDB access systems page, and users with Niagara HPSS access before August 5th were automatically opted-in.

qa_pairs.question: Is HPSS fully integrated with Trillium currently?
qa_pairs.answer: No, HPSS has not yet been integrated into Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What is the SSH command to log in to the Trillium CPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You can log in by using SSH to `USERNAME@trillium.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the SSH command to log in to the Trillium GPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: You can log in by using SSH to `USERNAME@trillium-gpu.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the login requirements for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to use SSH Keys and have MFA enabled on your CCDB account.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do the first time I log in to Trillium to ensure security?
qa_pairs.answer: You should check if the login node SSH host key fingerprint matches the one specified in the SSH security improvements documentation.

qa_pairs.question: What command should I run after logging in for the first time on Trillium to set up my environment?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run the command `trisetup`, which populates initialization files, creates directories, and sets up symbolic links.

qa_pairs.question: Will there be a web-based access option for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the SciNet OnDemand website will eventually offer a way to log into Trillium and run web-based applications like Jupyter.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key specifications of a Trillium CPU compute node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each CPU compute node has 192 AMD Zen 5 (Turin) cores and 755 GB of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: How many total CPU cores are available in the Trillium CPU subcluster?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 1224 compute nodes, providing a total of 235,008 cores.

qa_pairs.question: What are the key specifications of a Trillium GPU compute node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each GPU compute node has 96 AMD Zen 4 (Genoa) cores, 755 GB of available memory, and 4 NVIDIA H100 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: How many total GPUs does the Trillium cluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: There are 61 GPU compute nodes, totaling 244 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What are the recommended math libraries for CPU code compilation on Trillium if I previously used Intel MKL?
qa_pairs.answer: You should switch to Flexiblas or use the AMD AOCL libraries (`aocl-blas` and `aocl-lapack` modules) directly.

qa_pairs.question: What happened to my data from Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: Any data in your home, scratch, and project directories on Niagara on July 31st, 2025, that was unchanged afterward, was automatically copied to Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: How can I manually copy data from Niagara that was changed after the automatic migration date?
qa_pairs.answer: You can SSH to `nia-datamover1.scinet.utoronto.ca` and copy files locally between the mounted Niagara and Trillium file systems.

qa_pairs.question: What are the default quotas for home and scratch directories on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The default quotas are 100 GB for home and 25 TB for scratch.

qa_pairs.question: What are the new directory paths for my home and scratch directories on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Your home directory is at `/home/USERNAME` and your scratch directory is at `/scratch/USERNAME`.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find my group's project directory on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Your group's project directory is at `/project/RRG-NAME`, and all groups also have a default `/project/def-PINAME`.

qa_pairs.question: Which Trillium directories are backed up?
qa_pairs.answer: Home and project directories are backed up, but scratch is not.

qa_pairs.question: How do home directory permissions differ on Trillium compared to Niagara for sharing files?
qa_pairs.answer: On Trillium, your home directory files have group-ownership of your own private group, requiring ACLs or group ownership changes for sharing.

qa_pairs.question: Where should compute jobs write data on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Compute jobs must write to the scratch directory because home and project file systems are read-only on compute nodes.

qa_pairs.question: Which software stacks from Niagara and Mist are no longer available on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The NiaEnv and MistEnv software stacks have been retired and are not available on Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary software stack used on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The CVMFS software stack, available on other national clusters, is used on Trillium.

qa_pairs.question: Which modules are loaded by default on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Only the `gentoo/2023` and `CCconfig` modules are loaded by default.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load the standard set of modules, including a default compiler, on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You should load the `StdEnv/2023` module using `module load StdEnv/2023`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I load the CUDA module for GPU code compilation on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to load `StdEnv/2023` and then `cuda/12.6` using `module load StdEnv/2023 cuda/12.6`.

qa_pairs.question: Is recompilation of my code required on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is highly recommended to recompile all code, especially if it didn't use the CVMFS software stack or used absolute paths.

qa_pairs.question: Do I need to recreate my Python virtual environments on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Python virtual environments must be recreated due to changes in user directory paths.

qa_pairs.question: Is Anaconda available on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no Anaconda module; users should switch to using virtual environments instead.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a quickstart guide for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: A new quickstart guide is available at Trillium Quickstart.

qa_pairs.question: What is the support email address for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: The support email is `trillium@tech.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a self-guided introductory course for Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, there is an 'Intro to SciNet and Trillium' self-guided course available at https://education.scinet.utoronto.ca/course/view.php?id=1389.
